curation_policy.goal: Reduce hallucinations by storing small, source-linked recommendation facts instead of raw scraped pages.
rules: Prefer official NVIDIA sources for product capabilities.
Use case materials only for strategic framing, not for NVIDIA product claims.
If a startup signal is weak or absent, the recommendation must say evidence is insufficient.
Every recommendation fact must carry at least one source_id.
Do not infer partnership, eligibility, pricing, availability, or production readiness unless the evidence explicitly supports it.

case_emergence_ai_native_services.title: Emergence Capital - The AI-Native Services Playbook

case_nvidia_ai_5_layer_cake.title: NVIDIA Blog - AI Is a 5-Layer Cake

nvidia_clara.title: NVIDIA Clara / Healthcare and Life Sciences

frameworks.name: AI-native services
frameworks.summary: Use this frame when evaluating startups that turn service delivery into scalable software-like workflows with AI.
startup_signals: Manual expert service is being converted into a repeatable AI-assisted workflow.
The product uses agents, copilots, automation, or proprietary data to reduce delivery cost.
The company can explain how AI changes margin, speed, quality, or scalability.

frameworks.name: NVIDIA AI infrastructure layers
frameworks.summary: Use this frame to map a startup need to applications, models, infrastructure, chips, and energy constraints.
startup_signals: The startup has a bottleneck beyond app UX, such as inference, model customization, data pipelines, or GPU operations.
The recommendation should distinguish model layer needs from serving, infrastructure, and accelerated computing needs.

technologies.name: NVIDIA Inception
technologies.what_it_is: Program for startups in the NVIDIA ecosystem, relevant for technical resources, ecosystem access, visibility, and startup benefits.
recommend_when: The company is a startup building with AI, data science, accelerated computing, robotics, healthcare, cybersecurity, simulation, or related NVIDIA-aligned domains.
The team needs ecosystem support, technical enablement, partner access, or go-to-market visibility.
do_not_overclaim: Do not claim acceptance, eligibility, funding, credits, or specific benefits without direct evidence.

technologies.name: NVIDIA API Catalog
technologies.what_it_is: Catalog for exploring and testing NVIDIA-hosted AI APIs and models before deeper integration.
recommend_when: The startup is prototyping generative AI, multimodal, speech, vision, or agentic capabilities.
The team needs to compare model capabilities before deciding whether to self-host or deploy production infrastructure.
do_not_overclaim: Do not treat API exploration as proof that the startup has production-grade deployment.

technologies.name: NVIDIA NIM
technologies.what_it_is: Microservices for deploying optimized AI models, including generative AI models, with an operational serving path.
recommend_when: The startup needs to move AI models from experimentation to deployable inference services.
The product uses assistants, copilots, agents, multimodal models, or generative AI and needs lower operational friction.
do_not_overclaim: Do not recommend NIM only because a startup mentions AI; require evidence of model serving, inference, or generative AI usage.

technologies.name: NVIDIA NeMo
technologies.what_it_is: Suite for developing, customizing, evaluating, and operating generative AI and agentic systems.
recommend_when: The startup needs domain adaptation, customization, evaluation, or lifecycle tooling for generative AI.
The product depends on proprietary data, domain-specific assistants, voice, language, multimodal, or agent workflows.
do_not_overclaim: Do not recommend NeMo as a generic chatbot tool unless there is evidence of model lifecycle or customization needs.

technologies.name: NeMo Guardrails
technologies.what_it_is: Toolkit for adding programmable guardrails to LLM-based conversational systems.
recommend_when: The startup exposes assistants, copilots, chatbots, or agents to users.
The product needs topic control, policy enforcement, safer responses, or reduced off-scope behavior.
do_not_overclaim: Guardrails reduce risk but do not guarantee legal compliance, factual accuracy, or total safety.

technologies.name: NVIDIA Triton Inference Server
technologies.what_it_is: Inference server for deploying and serving models across multiple frameworks in production environments.
recommend_when: The startup serves multiple models or frameworks and needs a standardized inference layer.
The product needs scalable, observable, production-grade prediction APIs.
do_not_overclaim: Do not recommend Triton for teams that only consume third-party APIs and do not operate their own models.

technologies.name: NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM
technologies.what_it_is: Library for optimizing LLM inference on NVIDIA GPUs.
recommend_when: The startup self-hosts or plans to self-host LLMs and has latency, throughput, or cost-per-token constraints.
The product has high-volume generative inference requirements.
do_not_overclaim: Do not recommend TensorRT-LLM for basic API-only prototypes with no evidence of self-hosted LLM inference.

technologies.name: NVIDIA RAPIDS, cuDF, and cuML
technologies.what_it_is: GPU-accelerated data science stack for dataframe processing and classical machine learning workflows.
recommend_when: The startup has large tabular data pipelines, feature engineering, analytics, classical ML, or slow pandas/scikit-learn workflows.
The main bottleneck is data processing or traditional ML training, not only LLM prompting.
do_not_overclaim: Do not recommend RAPIDS only because a startup uses data; require volume, latency, or compute bottleneck evidence.

technologies.name: CUDA Toolkit
technologies.what_it_is: Development toolkit for building GPU-accelerated applications.
recommend_when: The startup develops custom high-performance workloads, simulation, numerical computing, computer vision, or low-level GPU acceleration.
The team has engineering capacity to optimize beyond high-level frameworks.
do_not_overclaim: Do not recommend CUDA as the first step for business teams without evidence of custom GPU development needs.

technologies.name: NVIDIA Riva
technologies.what_it_is: Speech AI SDK for building real-time speech and language applications.
recommend_when: The startup works with speech recognition, text-to-speech, real-time voice assistants, call centers, or voice agents.
do_not_overclaim: Do not recommend Riva for generic text-only chatbots.

technologies.name: NVIDIA Omniverse
technologies.what_it_is: Platform for simulation, digital twins, and connected 3D workflows.
recommend_when: The startup builds digital twins, industrial simulation, 3D collaboration, robotics simulation, or physical-world design workflows.
do_not_overclaim: Do not recommend Omniverse for ordinary dashboard or 2D SaaS products.

technologies.name: NVIDIA Isaac
technologies.what_it_is: Platform and tooling for robotics development, simulation, and autonomous machines.
recommend_when: The startup builds robots, autonomous systems, robotic perception, manipulation, AMRs, or robotics simulation.
do_not_overclaim: Do not recommend Isaac for software-only workflow automation with no robotics component.

technologies.name: NVIDIA Clara / Healthcare and Life Sciences
technologies.what_it_is: NVIDIA healthcare and life sciences ecosystem for accelerated computing and AI in medical and scientific workflows.
recommend_when: The startup works in medical imaging, healthcare AI, hospitals, life sciences, drug discovery, or clinical workflows.
do_not_overclaim: Do not imply regulatory approval or clinical validation without direct external evidence.

technologies.name: NVIDIA Morpheus
technologies.what_it_is: AI framework for cybersecurity analytics and threat detection workflows.
recommend_when: The startup analyzes security telemetry, logs, anomalies, SOC workflows, fraud signals, or threat detection at scale.
do_not_overclaim: Do not recommend Morpheus for general IT management without cybersecurity or anomaly detection evidence.

technologies.name: NVIDIA AI Enterprise
technologies.what_it_is: Enterprise software platform for developing and operating AI with support, security, and enterprise deployment considerations.
recommend_when: The startup sells to enterprise, regulated, or hybrid-cloud customers that need supported AI software and production governance.
The deployment context requires stability, support, security, or standardized AI operations.
do_not_overclaim: Do not recommend AI Enterprise only because the startup is B2B; require enterprise deployment or operational governance needs.
